mnemonics: JAVA JDK, Android SDK, AVD: Just Start Developing Awesome Virtual Devices
keyPoints: Install Java Development Kit (JDK) as it is required to run Android Studio.
Download and install Android Studio, the official IDE for Android development.
Use the SDK Manager within Android Studio to install the necessary platform tools and system images.
Create an Android Virtual Device (AVD) using the AVD Manager to emulate different Android devices for testing.
Ensure the environment variables (like ANDROID_HOME) are correctly set if needed for command-line tools.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to enable 'Virtualization Technology' (VT-x) in the computer's BIOS/UEFI settings, which is required for the Android Emulator to run.
Installing an incompatible version of the JDK that does not work with the current version of Android Studio.
